original: Damasus I begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Edgar I, the Peaceful, becomes king of all England
John XIII Crescentii elected to succeed Pope Leo VIII
Gerard de Ridefort, grandmaster of the Knights Templar since 1184, is killed in the Siege of Acre
Count Rudolf van Habsburg becomes the first King of the Germans
Storm breaks Leiden dike, drowning 20,000 Spanish soldiers and helping end the Siege of Leiden
Spanish troops under Spinola occupies fort Rhine birch
Russian parliament accepts annexation of Ukraine
Battle of Simbirsk: Russian Tsarist army decisively defeats a large force of rebel Cossacks led by Stepan Razin on the Volga River
Prince William of Orangeaccepts invitation to take up the British crown
Parliament declares Hungary independent, French Rákóczi becomes king
British troops under General Thomas Gage land in Boston
Russians under Suvorov defeat the Turks at Kinburn
First session of the new French legislative assembly
Belgium is conquered by France
France annexes Southern Netherlands
Spain cedes Louisiana to France in a secret treaty
Britain & France sign Preliminary of London
The Russian army under Ivan Paskevich storms Yerevan, ending a millennium of Muslim domination in Armenia
South African College is founded in Cape Town, later separates into the University of Cape Town and the South African College Schools
General Trade Journal newspaper begins publishing in Amsterdam
"Racer's" Hurricane in the Gulf of Mexico
US imposes treaty on Winnebago Indians in Wisconsin
News of the World begins publication in London
Maria Mitchell discovers a non-naked-eye comet
1st Hawaiian stamps issued

The watch company founded in 1850 in Roxbury by Aaron Lufkin Dennison relocates to Waltham, Massachusetts, to become the Waltham Watch Company, a pioneer in the American System of Watch Manufacturing.
5 Russian warships welcomed in NYC
Dutch Breda-Tilburg railway opens
Cyclone strikes Calcutta: 70,000 killed
John Summerfield Staples is paid $500 as a substitute for US President Abraham Lincoln
Dutch Eindhoven-Venlo railway opens
1st edition of Maasbode published
St Pancras railway station opens in London, largest single span roof in the world (689ft (240m) long, 100ft high (30m) a high point of Victorian Gothic architecture
1st postcards are issued in Vienna, Austria
General Dutch Werkliedenverbond (ANWV), forms in Utrecht
Rotterdam opens drinking water pipes
Cincinnati Enquirer publishes first report on Baseball's reserve clause; rights to players retained by team upon the contract's expiration; replaced by free agency
Special delivery mail service begins in US
US mint at Carson City, Nevada, closes
Balochistan (part of modern Pakistan) conquered by the British
National Geographic magazine is published for the first time
Dutch soccer club HFC Haarlem is founded; National champions 1946; bankrupt in 2010; merge with HFC Kennemerland, new club Haarlem Kennemerland
Washington voters adopt state constitution in referendum
Germany's anti-socialist laws lapse
US Congress creates Weather Bureau
US Congress establishes Yosemite National Park (California)
University of Chicago opens
Civic organization, Knights of Ak-Sar-Ben founded in Omaha, Nebraska
First meeting of The Owl Club of Cape Town
Romanians in Costantinople massacred
Dutch railway Alkmaar-Receiver opens
Henry Huntington buys LA Railway
Jews are expelled from Kyiv, Ukraine
The Vienna University of Economics and Business Administration is founded under the name k.u.k. Exportakademie.
Tsar Nikolay II expels Jews from major Russian cities
JB van Heutsz becomes governor general of Dutch East Indies
Netherlands and Portugal lay down boundaries splitting the island of Timor in two
Plaza Hotel at 5th Avenue & 59th Street opens in New York
1st Dutch electric railway in use (Rotterdam-The Hague)
Berkshire Cattle Fair held in Pittsfield Mass (1st state fair)
Explosion at LA Times kills 21
Regina Rugby Club (to become CFL Saskatchewan Roughriders) plays first game, losing to Moose Jaw Tigers, 16-6
United Dutch Diamond workers get 8-hr day
The first division of Canadian troops, 33,000 sail for Britain; most Canadians are volunteers, anxious to prove their loyalty to the Commonwealth
The Prince of Asir, the southern region of Arabia, rises in revolt against the Turks; his actions will inspire other Arab leaders to revolt
Dutch law provides for an 8 hour working day
Sir Percy Cox landed in Basra to assume his responsibilities as high commissioner in Iraq.
WJZ, Newark NJ begins broadcasting
Former Chicago Staleys play first NFL game as Chicago Bears; beat Racine Legion, 6-0 at Horlick Field, Racine, Wisconsin
Pittsburgh Pirates with a team including 5 future Baseball Hall of Famers clinch NL pennant with 9-6 win v Cincinnati Reds; Pirates' last NL pennant until 1960
Finnish distance runnerPaavo Nurmiruns 15k world record 46:49.6 and 10 miles 50:15.0 in Berlin, Germany
Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) opens in the Westchester section of Los Angeles California
USSR launches its first 5-year plan
Spanish pianist José Iturbi makes his US debut with Leopold Stokowski and The Philadelphia Orchestra at the Academy of Music in Philadelphia
Spanish Cortes accept general female suffrage
The second (and current) Waldorf-Astoria Hotel is opened in New York.
Washington Senators coach Nick Altrock plays in a MLB game at age 57 as a pinch-hitter; faces Rube Walberg of the Philadelphia A's; goes hitless in 3-0 loss
Pullman Company formally recognizes Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters
After a one-month Siege of Warsaw, hostile forces entered the city.
Pennsylvania Turnpike, pioneer toll thruway, opens
Bell P-59 Airacomet fighter, 1st US jet, makes maiden flight
Little Golden Books (children's books) begins publishing
1st King's Dragoon Guards (Royal Amoured Corps) liberates suburbs of Naples
Allied forces captured Naples during WW II
Averell Harriman named US ambassador to Moscow
Germans attack Jews in Denmark
Newspaper editor Alejandro Córdova assassinated in Guatemala
St Louis Browns beat New York Yankees 5-2 at Sportsman's Park III to win their only AL pennant; lose World Series 4-2 to neighbour Cardinals
US Office of Strategic Services (OSS), precursor to the CIA, disbands
12 Nazi war criminals sentenced to death in Nuremberg
St. Louis Cardinals beats Brooklyn Dodgers, 4-2 in first MLB play-off game for a league championship (NL); St Louis wins series, 2-0
1st helicopter air mail & express service, LA, Ca
The F-86 Sabre flies for the first time.
US control of Haitian customs & governmental revenue ends
William Wister Haines' "Command Decision" premieres in NYC
California Supreme Court voids state statute banning interracial marriages, in Perez v. Sharp case
Radio Denmark begins transmitting
Publication of poetry volume "Annie Allen" by Gwendolyn Books, - 1950 Pulitzer Prize for poetry and first African American recipient of a Pulitzer
Republic of China (Taiwan) forms on island of Formosa
Philadelphia Phillies clinch NL pennant on Dick Sisler's 3-run home run in a season ending 4-1 win v Brooklyn Dodgers; Phillies first pennant since 1913
South Korean troops cross the 38th parallel into North Korea
1st treaty signed by woman ambassador-Eugenie Anderson (US ambassador to Denmark)
24th Infantry Regiment, last all-black US military unit, deactivated
1st ultra high frequency (UHF) television station, Portland Or
Liberal Party wins Japanese elections
Monte Bello-Island (Great Britain 1st atom bomb explosion)
Indian state of Andhra Pradesh partitioned from Madras
KJEO TV channel 47 in Fresno, CA (CBS/ABC) begins broadcasting
KYTV TV channel 3 in Springfield, MO (NBC) begins broadcasting
WATE TV channel 6 in Knoxville, TN (ABC/NBC) begins broadcasting
WREX TV channel 13 in Rockford, IL (ABC) begins broadcasting
British colony of Nigeria becomes a federation
Baltimore Colts fullback Alan Ameche becomes the first rookie in NFL history to top 100 yards rushing in his first 2 games; totals 153 against Detroit Lions after 194 yards in debut v Chicago Bears
Piet Lieftinck becomes director v/h IMF (World bank)
WORA TV channel 5 in Mayaguez, PR (TCI) begins broadcasting
American jockey Johnny Heckmann becomes first to ride 7 winners at Hawthorne Race Course at Stickney/Cicero near Chicago, Illinois
Zestienhoven airport at Rotterdam official opens
B-52 bombers begin full-time flying alert in case of USSR attack
First appearance of "In God We Trust" on U.S. paper currency.
Britain transfers Christmas Island (south of Java) to Australia
US space agency NASA begins operations incorporating earlier National Advisory Council on Aeronautics and other bodies
KCBY TV channel 11 in Coos Bay, OR (CBS) begins broadcasting
Nigeria gains independence from the United Kingdom (National Day) and becomes the 99th member of the United Nations
A volcanco believed to be extinct erupts in Tristan da Cunha
East & West Cameroon merge as Federal Republic of Cameroon
KGIN TV channel 11 in Grand Island, NB (CBS) begins broadcasting
Premiere ofDmitri Shostakovich's 12nd Symphony
The Beach Boys record their debut single "Surfin", introducing a new musical style
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
WOLO TV channel 25 in Columbia, SC (ABC) begins broadcasting
WYAH TV channel 27 in Portsmouth, VA (IND) begins broadcasting
UN gives Netherlands control of New Guinea
US National Radio Astronomy Observatory installs a 300' (91m) radio telescope in Green Bank, West Virginia
WEDH TV channel 24 in Hartford, Connecticut (PBS) begins broadcasting
Nigeria enacts a new constitution and becomes a republic
1st official broadcast of Trans World Radio on Bonaire
2nd Dutch televisienet begins broadcastings
Free Speech Movement launched at University of California, Berkley
Japan's first bullet train (Tokaido Shinkansen) departs Tokyo for Osaka cutting the 6.5 hour journey to 4 hours
San Francisco cable cars declared a national landmark
WSJU TV channel 18 in Carolina, PR begins broadcasting
WTSJ TV channel 18 in San Juan, PR (NBC) begins broadcasting
WWAY TV channel 3 in Wilmington, NC (ABC) begins broadcasting
Failed coup under lt col Untung in Indonesia
France performs underground nuclear test at Ecker Algeria
Newspaper magnate Thomson purchases "The Times"
WAEO (now WJFW) TV channel 12 in Rhinelander, WI (NBC) begins
West Coast Airlines Flight 956 crashes with eighteen fatal injuries and no survivors 5.5 miles south of Wemme, Oregon. This accident marks the first loss of a DC-9.
KBFI (now KDAF) TV channel 33 in Dallas-Fort Worth, TX (IND) begins
Alexander the Greatdefeats Darius III of Persia in the Battle of Gaugamela
Conciliabulum of Pisa opens convened byLouis XIIof France
Meeting betweenMartin Lutherand Huldrych Zwingli
Duke of Norfolk arrested byQueen Elizabeth Ifor conspiring to marry MaryQueen of Scots
Treaty of Raalte: makesWilliam IIIPrince of Orange no longer Stadtholder of Overijssel province
Yachting begins in England as KingCharles IIbeats his brother James, Duke of York in a yacht race from Greenwich to Gravesend
Scotland'sBonnie Prince Charlieflees to France
Battle of Rancaguabegins; Spanish army defeats rebel Chilean army, ending the Chilean Vieja
Opening of the Congress of Vienna, redraws Europe's political map after the defeat of Napoléon Bonaparte

German explorerLudwig Leichhardtdeparts Jimbour, the farthest outpost of settlement on the Queensland Darling Downs, to begin his exploration of Australia's Northern Territory from Moreton Bay to Port Essington
German inventor and industrialistWerner von Siemensfounds Siemens AG & Halske
Alfred Russel Wallacereturns to the U.K. after four years of travel, collecting, and research in South America
Karl Marxpublishes "Das Kapital" in Berlin, a description of the capitalist system, its instability and tendency to self-destruction
"Little Women" byLouisa May Alcottis published in America by Roberts Brothers of Boston
John Philip Sousabecomes new director of US Marine Corps Band

Stanford University, California opens its doors after being founded byLeland Stanfordand his wife Jane with a $40M donation (1891 dollars), in memory of their son. Among its first graduation class, future US PresidentHerbert Hoover.
František Pavlík is killed in a demonstration in Prague, inspiringLeoš Janáčekto write his Piano Sonata 1. X. 1905.
A downturn in the stock market leads to a run on the dollar; US PresidentTheodore Rooseveltlater calls on financierJ. P. Morganto help manage the financial crisis
Future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherJack Chesbrorecords final victory for the NY Highlanders before waived and claimed by Red Sox; beatsWalter Johnsonand Washington Senators, 2-1
World War I: Combined Arab and British force underT. E. Lawrence, known asLawrence of Arabia, captures Damascus from the Turks
St. Louis Cardinals 2nd basemanRogers Hornsbyhits 3-for-5 in 7-1 regular season ending win v Chicago Cubs; improves batting average to .401; only MLB player to bat .400 and hit 40 HRs in same season
1st Dutch airliner, theFokker F.VIItakes off from Amsterdam for Batavia (Java)
Finnish distance runnerPaavo Nurmiruns world record 4 mile (19:15.4) & 5 mile (24:06.2) at Vilpuri, Russia
Oil field accident costs aviatorWiley Posthis left eye, uses the settlement money to buy his 1st aircraft
Oswald Mosleyforms British Union of Fascists
Babe Ruthmakes his final pitching appearance in MLB; pitches all 9 innings in a season ending 6-5 win v Boston Red Sox at Yankee Stadium; hits his 34th home run of the season in 5th inning
New York Giants make no 1st downs, but still beat the Green Bay Packers, 10-7 in a week 3 NFL matchup at Borchert Field, Milwaukee, Wisconsin
Adolf Hitlerexpands German army and navy, violating Treaty of Versailles
GeneralissimoFrancisco Francoestablishes state of Spain
Germany annexes Sudetenland (1/3 of Czechoslovakia) following the Munich Agreement
Winston Churchillcalls Russia a "riddle wrapped in a mystery inside an enigma"
Dmitri Shostakovichevacuated by plane to Moscow
World heavyweight boxing championJoe Louisis discharged from US army after being awarded the Legion of Merit
Cleveland Indians pitcherBob Fellerclaims his MLB record 348th strikeout of season in a 4-1 win over Detroit; record stands for 19 years
People's Republic of China proclaimed byMao Zedong(National Day)
Connie Mack's last game as Philadelphia Athletics manager (1901-50); longest serving manager in MLB history; beat Washington Senators, 5-3 at Shribe Park
Jackie Gleason's sitcom "The Honeymooners" debuts on CBS, replacing his variety series; production ends after 39 episodes
Wole Soyinka's play "A Dance of the Forests" is first performed as part of Nigerian Independence celebrations
New York Yankees outfielderRoger Marisbecomes MLB’s all-time season home run leader when he passes Babe Ruth's mark with No. 61 off Boston rookie Tracy Stallard in 1-0 win at Yankee Stadium
American singerBarbra Streisandsigns her 1st recording contract (with Columbia)
Brian Epsteinsigns a revised contract to manage The Beatles through 1977
James Meredithenrols at the University of Mississippi, becoming its first black student, guarded by hundreds of federal troops after the Ole Miss riot
Johnny Carsonhosts his 1st "The Tonight Show" on NBC; guests include Rudy Vallée,Tony Bennett,Mel BrooksandJoan Crawford
Sitcom "The Lucy Show" starringLucille ball, the follow-up to "I Love Lucy" premieres on CBS
Pink Floydarrive in New York for their first US tour
Richard Pettycontinues phenomenal NASCAR winning streak by taking the Wilkes 400 at North Wilkesboro Speedway; unprecedented 10th consecutive victory
Roger Sessions' 7th Symphony premieres in Ann Arbor, Michigan with Jean Martinon conducting the Chicago Symphony Orchestra
Cult zombie film "Night of the Living Dead," directed byGeorge A. Romeroand starring Duane Jones and Judith O'Dea, premieres in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
Henry Fordintroduces the Model T car, priced at $825
